name: LightFM (Hybrid Recommendation Algorithm)

performance.notes: C extension with OpenMP parallelism; training scales well with n_jobs

agent_readiness.notes: Hybrid matrix factorization supporting both collaborative and content-based features. from lightfm import LightFM. from lightfm.data import Dataset. dataset = Dataset(). dataset.fit(users, items, user_features=[...], item_features=[...]). interactions, weights = dataset.build_interactions(data). model = LightFM(loss='warp', no_components=64). model.fit(interactions, user_features=uf, item_features=if_, epochs=30, num_threads=4). scores = model.predict(user_ids, item_ids). Losses: warp, bpr, logistic, warp-kos. Cold-start via item/user features. Mostly unmaintained since 2022.

gotchas: Largely unmaintained since 2022 — issues pile up without fixes
No type annotations — poor IDE experience
Data preparation via Dataset API is verbose and error-prone
Sparse matrix format differs from implicit/scipy conventions
best_when: Hybrid recommendation with both user/item side features and interactions
Cold-start scenarios where new user/item metadata is available
Small to medium datasets where GPU acceleration is not needed
avoid_when: Large-scale recommendations (use implicit for pure CF at scale)
Active projects requiring maintained library (largely abandoned)
When only implicit feedback without side features is available
summary: LightFM implements hybrid recommendation combining collaborative filtering with content-based features. LightFM(loss='warp').fit(interactions, user_features=uf, item_features=if_). Dataset API builds sparse matrices from raw data. Supports cold-start via side features. Losses: WARP, BPR, logistic. Apache-2.0. C extension with OpenMP. Largely unmaintained since 2022 — for new projects prefer implicit (pure CF) or Cornac (active hybrid library).
